specification.topic-statement: The response line buffer accumulates incoming streaming text chunks, flushes complete lines to the scrollback with a model prefix on the first line, and extracts response metadata and tool calls at completion.

scope.in-scope: Text delta accumulation in the full response string and in the pending line buffer, newline-triggered line flushing to the scrollback, first-line model prefix insertion, usage event rendering, response metadata storage, and tool call and thinking block extraction from the completed response.
scope.boundary: Complete lines are appended to the scrollback by this topic. The scrollback manages its own storage.
The content block array of the completed response is read by this topic for tool call and thinking block extraction. The provider constructs and owns the response.

field.name: IK_CONTENT_THINKING
field.note: stores text and signature into pending thinking fields

field.name: IK_CONTENT_REDACTED_THINKING
field.note: stores encrypted data into pending redacted field

field.name: IK_CONTENT_TOOL_CALL
field.note: creates tool call record; loop stops after first

behavior.text-delta-accumulation:
When a text chunk arrives, if the chunk is empty, the operation returns immediately.
The chunk is appended to the agent's full response string. If no response string exists yet, it is initialized from the chunk. If appending fails due to out-of-memory, the process aborts.
The chunk is then scanned byte-by-byte for newline characters. For each newline found, the segment from the previous newline position (or chunk start) to the newline is flushed as a complete scrollback line. After all newlines, any remaining content after the last newline is appended to the pending line buffer. If the buffer does not exist yet, it is initialized from the trailing segment; otherwise it is extended.
behavior.line-flushing:
A segment is flushed to the scrollback as follows:
If the streaming-first-line flag is set and the model text output prefix exists (the "●" symbol), the prefix followed by a space is prepended to the line content. The combined string (prefix + space + any pending buffer content + segment) is appended to the scrollback as one line. The streaming-first-line flag is cleared after the first flush.
Lines after the first are appended directly: if a pending line buffer exists, it is prepended to the segment and the combined string is appended to the scrollback.
If the segment is empty and the first-line flag is set, the flush is deferred (no line is appended and no prefix is emitted). The prefix will be applied to the next non-empty line.
If the segment is empty and the first-line flag is not set, an empty line is appended to the scrollback.
After each flush the pending line buffer is freed and set to null.
behavior.usage-event-rendering: The agent's input, output, and thinking token counts are summed. If the total is greater than zero, a usage event is rendered to the scrollback as a JSON object containing all three counts. If the total is zero or less, an empty line is appended instead.
behavior.response-metadata-storage: Previous response model and finish reason strings are freed. The response model string from the provider response is copied into agent-owned memory. The finish reason enum is mapped to a string using the mapping in the data contract above and copied into agent-owned memory. Token counts (input, output, thinking) are copied from the response usage structure into the agent's numeric fields. If any string copy fails due to out-of-memory, the process aborts.
behavior.tool-call-extraction:
All pending thinking and tool call fields on the agent are cleared (freed if non-null, set to null). The response's content block array is then iterated:
- A thinking block stores its text and signature (each independently, each may be null) into the agent's pending thinking fields. - A redacted thinking block stores its encrypted data into the agent's pending redacted data field. - A tool call block creates a tool call record (copying ID, name, and arguments) and stores it as the agent's pending tool call. If a thought signature is present on the block, it is stored in the agent's pending tool thought signature field. Iteration stops after the first tool call block.
Only the first tool call content block is processed. Any additional tool call blocks in the same response are silently ignored.
